pi_items.source: NIST Chemistry WebBook lists 353 K ± 1 K = 79.85-80.85°C
NIST Chemistry WebBook lists 355.5 K ± 0.4 K = 82.35°C
NIST Chemistry WebBook lists 110.6°C for toluene
NIST Chemistry WebBook: m-xylene 412.3 K = 139.15°C, p-xylene 138.3°C, o-xylene 144°C. Mixed isomers range 138-144°C with 139°C as representative value.
Standard tolerance for boiling point measurements; reasonable given literature variations

Delta between MEK (80°C) and IPA (82°C) is 2°C, the smallest gap among adjacent chemicals when sorted by boiling point: Acetone(56)→MEK(80) Δ24, MEK(80)→IPA(82) Δ2 [CRITICAL], IPA(82)→Toluene(111) Δ29, Toluene(111)→Xylene(139) Δ28
Verified from CAMEO Chemicals and NIST WebBook data

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims for the distillation task are verified against authoritative sources. The five chemicals have boiling points (in Celsius): Acetone 56.1°C, Methyl Ethyl Ketone 80°C, Isopropyl Alcohol 82.35°C, Toluene 110.6°C, and Xylene 139°C. When sorted by boiling point (lowest to highest), the critical separation pair with the smallest temperature difference is Methyl Ethyl Ketone and Isopropyl Alcohol with a delta of 2°C. All boiling point values are sourced from CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA) and NIST Chemistry WebBook, which are based on real chemical data, not synthetic values.
